Moonpig Group Plc ( (GB:MOON) ) has issued an update.

Moonpig Group plc has continued to execute its share repurchase programme of up to £30m, buying back and cancelling ordinary shares between 20 and 24 April 2026 at prices around 209p to 217p per share through broker RBC Europe. Following these transactions, the company’s share count with voting rights has fallen to 306,318,316, a reduction that may enhance earnings per share and slightly increase existing shareholders’ proportional ownership.

The most recent analyst rating on (GB:MOON) stock is a Buy with a £285.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Moonpig Group Plc stock, see the GB:MOON Stock Forecast page.

Spark’s Take on MOON Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, MOON is a Neutral.

Moonpig Group Plc’s overall score is driven by positive technical indicators and strategic corporate actions, such as share buybacks and leadership changes. However, financial performance concerns, particularly around profitability and leverage, weigh heavily on the score. The valuation also suggests caution due to a negative P/E ratio.

To see Spark’s full report on MOON stock, click here.

More about Moonpig Group Plc

Moonpig Group plc is a leading online greeting cards and gifting platform, operating the Moonpig, Red Letter Days and Buyagift brands in the UK and the Greetz brand in the Netherlands. Its offering combines a wide range of cards and curated gifts with personalisation tools and next-day delivery, delivered via proprietary technology platforms and apps that use data science to tailor and scale the customer experience.

Funding Circle Holdings ( (GB:FCH) ) has shared an update.

Funding Circle Holdings plc has continued its previously announced share buy-back programme, repurchasing ordinary shares on the London Stock Exchange between 20 and 24 April 2026 at volume-weighted average prices ranging from about 152p to 158p per share. The transactions, executed via Investec Bank, reflect the company’s ongoing capital management strategy as it seeks to optimise its balance sheet and signal confidence in its longer-term prospects.

The repurchased shares are being held in treasury, bringing the company’s treasury holding to 6,266,728 ordinary shares and leaving 298,474,848 shares in issue that carry voting rights. This change in free float and voting rights structure is relevant for existing and prospective shareholders, who may use the updated share count as a reference for regulatory disclosure thresholds and to assess the potential impact of the buy-backs on earnings per share and ownership percentages.

The most recent analyst rating on (GB:FCH) stock is a Buy with a £300.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Funding Circle Holdings stock, see the GB:FCH Stock Forecast page.

Spark’s Take on FCH Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, FCH is a Neutral.

The score is driven primarily by mixed financials: a strong 2025 rebound in revenue and profitability is outweighed by continued cash burn and a sharp rise in leverage. Technically, the stock shows weak momentum (below key moving averages with negative MACD), while valuation is a relative positive with a low P/E.

To see Spark’s full report on FCH stock, click here.

Funding Circle Holdings plc is a UK-based financial services company that operates an online lending platform, connecting small and medium-sized enterprises with investors seeking exposure to SME loans. Listed on the London Stock Exchange, the group focuses on providing alternative finance to businesses that may be underserved by traditional banks.

Cyberoo S.p.A. ( (IT:CYB) ) has issued an announcement.

Cyberoo has continued to execute its share buyback program, acquiring 8,302 treasury shares on Euronext Growth Milan between 20 and 24 April 2026 at a weighted average price of €1.142, for a total outlay of €9,486.81. Following these purchases, the group now holds 536,311 treasury shares, signalling ongoing capital management activity that may support share liquidity and reflect management’s confidence in the company’s valuation.

The transactions were carried out through MIT SIM as authorized intermediary, with daily purchases spread over the trading week to comply with applicable EU market abuse and buyback regulations. The latest round adds to a series of buyback updates issued since August, underlining Cyberoo’s systematic approach to its repurchase plan and offering investors greater transparency on how the program is progressing over time.

More about Cyberoo S.p.A.

Cyberoo S.p.A. is an innovative Italian SME listed on Euronext Growth Milan that specializes in cybersecurity solutions for businesses. The company focuses on mid-sized enterprises, offering a broad portfolio of advanced technologies designed to protect, monitor and manage corporate IT ecosystems at price points aligned with this segment’s spending capacity.

Restore ( (GB:RST) ) has provided an announcement.

Restore plc has repurchased a total of 170,000 ordinary shares of 5 pence each on AIM between 20 and 24 April 2026, paying volume-weighted average prices ranging from about 244p to 256p per share through Investec Bank. The company plans to cancel these shares, which will reduce its total number of ordinary shares in issue to 136,187,876, slightly enhancing earnings per share and signaling ongoing active management of its capital base.

The buyback and cancellation marginally shrink Restore’s equity float, which can support per-share metrics and potentially underpin the share price. For existing shareholders, the move indicates confidence in the company’s valuation and provides a modest increase in ownership percentage without requiring additional investment.

Tong Ren Tang Technologies Co ( (HK:1666) ) just unveiled an announcement.

Tong Ren Tang Technologies has convened its 2025 annual general meeting for 12 June 2026 in Beijing, where shareholders will review the audited 2025 financial statements and the board’s annual report. The company will also seek approval for a final dividend of RMB0.18 per share, signalling confidence in its earnings and providing a cash return to investors.

Shareholders will vote on reappointing Ernst & Young and Ernst & Young Hua Ming LLP as overseas and domestic auditors for 2026, as well as amendments to meeting rules and the articles of association. The board is further asking for a general mandate to issue up to 20% additional shares, which would give management flexibility to raise capital and adjust the company’s capital structure as needed.

More about Tong Ren Tang Technologies Co

Tong Ren Tang Technologies Co. Ltd. is a Mainland China-based pharmaceutical company specialising in traditional Chinese medicine products. Listed in Hong Kong, it focuses on manufacturing and distributing herbal remedies and health supplements, serving both domestic and international markets through retail and wholesale channels.

data.title: Man Yue Technology sets June 2026 AGM to approve accounts, director re-elections and share issue mandate
data.description:
Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Man Yue Technology Holdings Ltd ( (HK:0894) ) has issued an update.

Man Yue Technology Holdings Ltd has called its annual general meeting for 26 June 2026 in Hong Kong, where shareholders will receive and adopt the audited financial statements for the year ended 31 December 2025 and the related directors’ and auditor’s reports. The agenda includes re-election of executive director Chan Yu Ching, Eugene and executive director Chan Lok Yan, Lorraine, as well as long-serving independent non-executive director Lo Kwok Kwei, David, together with authorisation for the board to determine directors’ remuneration and the re-appointment of KPMG as auditor.

Shareholders will also vote on a general mandate allowing the board to issue additional shares or dispose of treasury shares, subject to a cap of 15 per cent of the existing share capital and in line with Hong Kong’s listing requirements. This mandate, if approved, would enhance the company’s financial flexibility for potential capital raising or corporate actions over the coming year, while also reaffirming corporate governance arrangements through director re-elections and auditor continuity.

More about Man Yue Technology Holdings Ltd

Man Yue Technology Holdings Ltd is a Hong Kong–listed company incorporated in Bermuda and traded on the Main Board of the Stock Exchange of Hong Kong. The group operates in the technology and electronics sector, with its shares subject to the Listing Rules of Hong Kong, and is governed by a board comprising executive and independent non-executive directors.

Average Trading Volume: 382,360

Technical Sentiment Signal: Buy

Current Market Cap: HK$369.1M

data.title: Hainan Meilan Airport Sets 2026 AGM, Proposes No 2025 Final Dividend and New Share Mandate
data.description:
Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

An announcement from Hainan Meilan International Airport Company ( (HK:0357) ) is now available.

Hainan Meilan International Airport Company Limited has convened its annual general meeting for 21 May 2026 in Haikou to review the board and supervisory committee work reports, approve the audited financial statements for 2025 and set the 2026 remuneration for directors and supervisors. The board is also proposing not to distribute a final dividend for 2025, a move that preserves cash within the business and may signal a focus on funding operations or investment needs over near-term shareholder payouts.

Shareholders will vote on reappointing Pan-China Certified Public Accountants LLP as auditors for another term, maintaining continuity in the company’s audit oversight and financial reporting. In addition, the board is seeking a renewed general mandate to issue up to 20% of the existing domestic shares and 20% of the H shares, subject to PRC laws and regulatory approval, which would give the company flexibility to raise equity capital and adjust its capital structure if required.

The most recent analyst rating on (HK:0357) stock is a Sell with a HK$7.30 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Hainan Meilan International Airport Company stock, see the HK:0357 Stock Forecast page.

More about Hainan Meilan International Airport Company

Hainan Meilan International Airport Company Limited is a PRC-based joint stock company that operates Meilan International Airport in Haikou, Hainan Province. Listed in Hong Kong, it provides airport-related services and infrastructure, serving as a key regional aviation hub supporting passenger and cargo traffic for Hainan’s tourism- and trade-focused economy.

The company’s operations encompass airport management, ground handling and associated commercial services to airlines and travelers.

Logory Logistics Technology Co. Ltd. Class H ( (HK:2482) ) has issued an announcement.

Logory Logistics Technology Co., Ltd. has called its annual general meeting for May 28, 2026, in Hefei, where shareholders will review and approve the 2025 annual report, board and supervisory board work reports, the 2025 profit distribution plan, and the re-appointment of auditors.
